name: Visual Lifestyle
summary: Photo-led consumer storytelling with friendly type, generous image zones, soft cards, and simple caption-first composition.

style_dna.philosophy: Let the product, person, place, or image carry emotion; UI should frame the story.
style_dna.visual_signature: Large image blocks, warm accent, friendly captions, rounded media, and direct headlines.
style_dna.surface_logic: Warm or white background with full-bleed visual moments.

palette.accent_policy: Use accent color to orient the viewer; let images provide most color.
palette.semantic_policy: Avoid dashboard semantics unless catalog content requires availability or price states.

layout.grid: Image-first layouts, large hero crop plus short copy blocks.
layout.image_treatment: Full-bleed or large rounded photography with clear safe overlays.

components.cards: Image cards with short caption and soft border.
components.tables: Avoid unless catalog specs are necessary.
components.callouts: Caption panels, testimonial cards, and product notes.

slides.accent_rule: Pair friendly accent color with generous image areas and simple captions.

document.heading_rule: Use image captions and soft section markers; keep long docs light.

source_inheritance.method: Synthesized from local getdesign.md working corpus.

do: Reserve large safe areas for imagery.
Let photography or product visuals provide color range.
dont: Do not overlay small text on busy images.
Do not build text-heavy slides in this mode.
Do not use abstract stock visuals when real product/place imagery is needed.

pptx: Use deterministic HTML/CSS or PPTX text boxes for final text.
Keep one core idea per slide and validate rendered PNG dimensions.
docx: Translate visual style into Word typography, margins, heading hierarchy, tables, and callouts.
Keep documents printable unless the user explicitly requests PDF-only visual art direction.
carousel: Use high-resolution fixed canvas, mobile-readable text, and safe margins.
Make each frame complete enough to stand alone.
Reserve a large image or cinematic negative-space zone before adding text.
